args.task: What is the single biggest risk of spawning sub-agents as child processes?
args.context: We use piSpawn() to fork pi processes for isolated sub-agent execution.

content.text: the biggest risk is **security boundary collapse**: a spawned pi child usually inherits the parent’s permissions (fs access, env vars, tokens, network), so a compromised/mis-prompted sub-agent can read/exfiltrate secrets or mutate the workspace just as broadly as the main agent unless you explicitly sandbox/strip capabilities.

details.task: What is the single biggest risk of spawning sub-agents as child processes?

content.text:
Task: What is the single biggest risk of spawning sub-agents as child processes?

Context: We use piSpawn() to fork pi processes for isolated sub-agent execution.

the biggest risk is **security boundary collapse**: a spawned pi child usually inherits the parent’s permissions (fs access, env vars, tokens, network), so a compromised/mis-prompted sub-agent can read/exfiltrate secrets or mutate the workspace just as broadly as the main agent unless you explicitly sandbox/strip capabilities.

the biggest risk is **security boundary collapse**: a spawned pi child usually inherits the parent’s permissions (fs access, env vars, tokens, network), so a compromised/mis-prompted sub-agent can read/exfiltrate secrets or mutate the workspace just as broadly as the main agent unless you explicitly sandbox/strip capabilities.

content.text:
Task: What is the single biggest risk of spawning sub-agents as child processes?

Context: We use piSpawn() to fork pi processes for isolated sub-agent execution.

the biggest risk is **security boundary collapse**: a spawned pi child usually inherits the parent’s permissions (fs access, env vars, tokens, network), so a compromised/mis-prompted sub-agent can read/exfiltrate secrets or mutate the workspace just as broadly as the main agent unless you explicitly sandbox/strip capabilities.

content.text: the biggest risk is **security boundary collapse**: a spawned pi child usually inherits the parent’s permissions (fs access, env vars, tokens, network), so a compromised/mis-prompted sub-agent can read/exfiltrate secrets or mutate the workspace just as broadly as the main agent unless you explicitly sandbox/strip capabilities.

details.task: What is the single biggest risk of spawning sub-agents as child processes?

content.text:
Task: What is the single biggest risk of spawning sub-agents as child processes?

Context: We use piSpawn() to fork pi processes for isolated sub-agent execution.

the biggest risk is **security boundary collapse**: a spawned pi child usually inherits the parent’s permissions (fs access, env vars, tokens, network), so a compromised/mis-prompted sub-agent can read/exfiltrate secrets or mutate the workspace just as broadly as the main agent unless you explicitly sandbox/strip capabilities.
